question_text: A hydroelectric power station generates electricity. What energy transfers occur?
correct_answer: Gravitational potential energy of water → kinetic energy as water falls → mechanical energy in turbine → electrical energy in generator
explanation: In a hydroelectric power station, water stored at height has gravitational potential energy. As it falls, this converts to kinetic energy. The moving water turns turbines (mechanical energy), which drives generators to produce electricity (electrical energy).
